paper_title: Subfossil Cladocera Analysis in Central American Waterbodies

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing subfossil Cladocera remains from sediment samples for taxonomic analysis.

steps.description: Subfossil Cladocera remains were collected from surface sediment samples and lake sediment cores in 56 waterbodies located in Mexico, Guatemala, El Salvador, and Honduras.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from specified waterbodies

steps.description: Fresh sediment samples (2 cm³) were taken and digested in 10% KOH solution.
steps.searchable_summary: digest sediment samples in KOH solution

steps.description: Samples were stirred with a magnetic stirrer to eliminate humic matter and treated with 10% HCl to remove carbonates.
steps.searchable_summary: stir samples to remove humic matter and treat with HCl

steps.description: Samples were flushed with distilled water and sieved through a 38 µm sieve.
The residue was transferred to a test tube filled with 5 ml or 10 ml of distilled water and stained with two drops of safranin.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer residue to test tube and stain with safranin

steps.description: For identification, between three to five slides (0.1 cm³) were examined under an Olympus microscope (equipped with 100-400x magnification).
steps.searchable_summary: examine slides under microscope for identification

steps.description: Microphotographs were taken using cellSens Standard software.
Taxonomic identification was established based on relevant literature.
